description_long: This object consists of three angular, faceted rocks clustered together, each with a polygonal, geometric appearance. The surfaces are matte and display a consistent reddish-brown coloration, reminiscent of sandstone or weathered urban stone. The largest rock forms the central mass, with two smaller rocks attached at its sides, creating a natural yet stylized grouping. The edges and faces are sharply defined, lacking any rounded or eroded features, suggesting recent placement or artificial shaping. The overall form is low and squat, with broad, flat surfaces that could support small objects. There are no visible markings, graffiti, or signs of wear on the surfaces.
description: A cluster of three angular, reddish-brown rocks with faceted, geometric surfaces and a matte finish. The rocks are grouped closely, forming a low, sturdy barrier.
description_view_0: The front view shows the largest rock with two smaller rocks attached, highlighting the sharp, polygonal faces and the clustered arrangement.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the broad, flat top surface of the largest rock, with the smaller rocks partially obscured behind it.
description_view_2: From this angle, the angularity and depth of the main rock are visible, with the smaller rock appearing tucked behind.
description_view_3: This view reveals all three rocks clearly, showing their relative sizes and the way they are grouped together.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
